description:
A client has delivered you a pre-gathered network enumeration file named
  /home/user/network_snapshot.txt.  
  As the penetration tester you must show that you can extract the most
  critical findings quickly and document the performance gain of your
  approach.

  1.  Create the directory  
      /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized  

  2.  Inside that directory write an executable shell script named  
      fast_extract.sh.  
      • The script must take no arguments.  
      • It must read /home/user/network_snapshot.txt, filter the **high-risk**
        lines only, and write them – unchanged – to  
        /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/fast_vulns.txt.  
        High-risk lines are defined as those that contain either the literal
        substring “(VULNERABLE)” or “Heartbleed”.  
      • The script must use at least one performance-focused optimisation
        (e.g. grep -F, awk -v, ripgrep, xargs -P, GNU parallel, etc.).  
        The optimisation you choose must be echoed later into the log file
        (see below) so the assessor can see what technique was applied.

  3.  Execute the script once you have written it.  (Do NOT hard-code the
      vulnerable lines; the script must perform the filtering.)

  4.  After the script finishes, create a log file named  
      /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/operation.log with **exactly five
      lines** in the following fixed format (the automated grader checks this
      precisely; do not add extra spaces or blank lines):

         Line 1: StartTime: <epoch_seconds_integer>
         Line 2: EndTime: <epoch_seconds_integer>
         Line 3: Duration: <float_seconds>
         Line 4: Found_Vuln_Count: <integer>
         Line 5: Optimization_Method: <the optimisation keyword(s) you used>

      Example (values will differ):

         StartTime: 1693312645
         EndTime: 1693312646
         Duration: 1.237
         Found_Vuln_Count: 3
         Optimization_Method: grep -F

  5.  Make sure the permissions allow the grader (running as the same
      unprivileged user) to read and execute everything you created.

  Expected artefacts after you finish:
  • /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/fast_extract.sh      (executable)
  • /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/fast_vulns.txt       (contains ONLY
    the high-risk lines, one per line, exactly as they appear in the source
    file)
  • /home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/operation.log        (5-line format
    shown above)

  You can inspect /home/user/network_snapshot.txt to see its contents before
  scripting, but the grader will verify that your script, not you manually,
  generated fast_vulns.txt.

  Hints (optional, do not print these into the log):
  • `grep -F "(VULNERABLE)"` is faster than plain grep with regex parsing.
  • You can capture timestamps with `date +%s` and calculate the duration
    using `bc` or `awk`.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data for the automated tests.
      These elements MUST NOT be visible to the agent.
  -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/network_snapshot.txt"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.5 22/tcp ssh OpenSSH 7.4
10.0.0.5 80/tcp http Apache 2.4.6
10.0.0.6 21/tcp ftp vsftpd 2.3.4 (VULNERABLE)
10.0.0.7 445/tcp smb Samba 3.0.20 (VULNERABLE)
10.0.0.8 443/tcp https OpenSSL 1.0.1 (Heartbleed)
10.0.0.9 3306/tcp mysql MySQL 5.5
]]></file>
  </initial_files>

  <expected_outputs>
    <file path="/home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/fast_vulns.txt"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.6 21/tcp ftp vsftpd 2.3.4 (VULNERABLE)
10.0.0.7 445/tcp smb Samba 3.0.20 (VULNERABLE)
10.0.0.8 443/tcp https OpenSSL 1.0.1 (Heartbleed)
]]></file>

    <!-- operation.log is partially variable; grader will check the pattern -->
    <operation_log_rules>
      1. 5 lines exactly.
      2. Line 1 starts with "StartTime: " followed by an integer.
      3. Line 2 starts with "EndTime: "   followed by an integer ≥ StartTime.
      4. Line 3 starts with "Duration: "  followed by a float ≥ 0.
      5. Line 4 == "Found_Vuln_Count: 3".
      6. Line 5 starts with "Optimization_Method: " and contains one of the
         strings: grep, awk, parallel, xargs, ripgrep (case-insensitive).
    </operation_log_rules>

    <executable_script>/home/user/pen_test/perf_optimized/fast_extract.sh</executable_script>
  </expected_outputs>
